title: L1 local prototype pipeline activation

minimum_validation: start a localhost HTTP server for the prototype artifact
response contains the prototype title and at least one generated workflow section
write validation report with artifact refs and digests
not_counted_as_prototype: mock plan without a locally viewable UI
provider output without generated runnable files

scope_in: local idea spec JSON schema and fixture
template-based prototype builder using local_tool provider_kind
generation of a local static HTML prototype artifact
runbook and manifest for the generated prototype
localhost validation of the generated prototype
optional devmasterctl intake of the idea task proposal before generation
scope_out: Codex CLI or Claude Code CLI daemonization
accounts, auth, payment, billing, multitenancy, compliance, or SaaS backend

proposed_sequence.purpose: Review this L1 activation proposal before implementing the prototype pipeline.
Implement a local template-based idea-to-runnable-prototype pipeline.
Review generated prototype validation evidence before any real provider or deploy activation.

task_proposal.goal: Implement the first local L1 idea-to-runnable-prototype pipeline using deterministic templates and localhost validation.
task_proposal.rationale: The daemon and CLI control surface are now validated. The next product proof is not more governance scaffolding; it is a local L1 pipeline that turns an idea into a viewable prototype artifact while keeping real providers and deploy out of scope.

acceptance_checks: Generated artifact is locally runnable and visible in a browser.
Validation report proves localhost HTTP 200 for the generated prototype.
Validation report includes artifact refs and digests.
Pipeline uses local_tool only and makes zero real provider calls.
No external repo, deploy, PR, production, account, payment, auth, or multitenancy side effect occurs.

risk_controls.risk: template builder is mistaken for true AI product generation
risk_controls.mitigation: Proposal labels Phase 9 as deterministic local_tool proof of pipeline semantics; real provider activation requires a later gate.

risk_controls.risk: Markdown or JSON output is accepted as a prototype
risk_controls.mitigation: Runnable prototype definition requires a locally served visible artifact with HTTP validation.

risk_controls.risk: L1 work drifts into L2 SaaS concerns
risk_controls.mitigation: Accounts, auth, payment, billing, multitenancy, compliance, deploy, and external repo mutation are explicit scope_out.

recommendation: Proceed to Gate M review. Do not implement the L1 prototype pipeline until Gate M confirms the local_tool-only scope and runnable artifact definition.
